name: Alaska

spec.method_detail: Stir with ice until well-chilled. Strain into chilled coupe.

history: Published in Jacques Straub's 'Drinks' in 1913. Why it's called Alaska is unclear — some suggest the 'wild' combination of gin and Chartreuse seemed as remote as Alaska. Yellow Chartreuse (milder than green) melds with gin in a surprisingly harmonious way. It's a lesser-known gem of the pre-Prohibition era.
